questions.question: A group of agents are communicating through a distributed system, each agent has its own local view of the system state. How can the agents agree on a global state, despite their individual views differing?
questions.hint: Consider the approaches of Distributed Mutual Exclusion and Distributed Agreement

questions.question: In a distributed system, two agents are trying to access a shared resource simultaneously. Using Distributed Mutual Exclusion, what is the outcome when one agent is trying to acquire the resource while the other is in a waiting state?
questions.hint: Consider the possible outcomes and the role of the token or priority queue

questions.question: A distributed system consists of multiple agents that need to agree on a common decision. What coordination mechanism can be used to ensure that all agents agree on the decision, and how does it handle cases where an agent fails to respond?
questions.hint: Consider the principles of Distributed Agreement and its variants
